title: Reduce: Simple Sum
description: Given an array of numbers, return the sum of all elements using `.reduce()`. Start with an initial value of 0.
whatShouldHappen: Use .reduce() on the input array.
The callback should take two arguments: `accumulator` and `currentValue`.
Add the `currentValue` to the `accumulator` in each step.
Provide 0 as the second argument to .reduce() (the initial value).
patternExplanation: The most basic use of reduce is 'boiling down' an array into a single numeric value like a sum or product.

title: Reduce: Long Sentence
description: Given an array of words, use `.reduce()` to concatenate them into a single string separated by spaces. Do not use `.join()`.
whatShouldHappen: Use .reduce() with an empty string `''` as the initial value.
In each step, append the current word to the accumulator.
Handle the space: return `acc + ' ' + curr` but only if `acc` is not empty.
patternExplanation: Reduce can build any type of value, including strings. This mimics the behavior of .join().

title: Reduce: Vote Counter
description: Given an array of strings representing votes (e.g., `['apple', 'banana', 'apple']`), return an object that tallies the count for each vote.
whatShouldHappen: Use .reduce() with an empty object `{}` as the initial value.
For each vote, increment its count in the accumulator object.
Return the accumulator object.
patternExplanation: Using an object as an accumulator allows you to count occurrences or group data by keys in a single pass.

title: Reduce: Group by Age
description: Given an array of user objects `{ name: string, age: number }`, return an object where the keys are ages and the values are arrays of names for that age.
whatShouldHappen: Initial value: `{}`.
If the age key doesn't exist, initialize it to `[]`.
Push the user's name into the appropriate age array.
patternExplanation: Reduce is incredibly powerful for re-structuring flat arrays into categorized or hierarchical objects.

title: Reduce: Flatten Arrays
description: Given an array of arrays, return a single 'flattened' array containing all the elements. Do not use `.flat()`.
whatShouldHappen: Use .reduce() with an initial value of an empty array `[]`.
Merge each sub-array into the accumulator using `.concat()`.
patternExplanation: Before .flat() was standard, reduce + concat was the idiomatic way to flatten nested structures.

title: Reduce: Find Maximum
description: Given an array of numbers, return the largest value using `.reduce()`. If the array is empty, return `null`.
whatShouldHappen: Use .reduce() without an initial value to use the first element as the starting max.
Compare `acc` and `curr` and return the larger one.
patternExplanation: Omitting the initial value is useful for operations like finding max/min where you want to start with a valid value from the dataset.

title: Reduce: Calculate Average
description: Given an array of numbers, return their average. If the array is empty, return 0.
whatShouldHappen: Sum all numbers using .reduce().
Divide the result by the array length.
patternExplanation: Calculations like average, variance, or standard deviation usually start with a reduction to find a sum.

title: Reduce: Create Lookup Map
description: Given an array of objects with unique IDs, return an object where each key is an ID and the value is the corresponding object.
whatShouldHappen: Use .reduce() with an initial value of `{}`.
Assign each item to the accumulator object using its ID.
patternExplanation: Indexing data is a common pattern for making data retrieval constant-time O(1) in the rest of your application.

title: Reduce: Single Pass Filter/Map
description: Given an array of names, return a new array containing the UPPERCASE version of names that are 4 characters or shorter. Use only ONE call to `.reduce()`.
whatShouldHappen: Initial value: `[]`.
Check length of current name.
If <= 4, convert to uppercase and push to accumulator.
Return the accumulator.
patternExplanation: Performing filter and map in a single reduce pass is a micro-optimization that avoids creating intermediate arrays.

title: Reduce: Find Longest Word
description: Given an array of words, return the longest word using `.reduce()`. If there's a tie, return the first one encountered.
whatShouldHappen: Use .reduce() without an initial value (or use `''` if preferred).
Compare lengths of accumulator and current word.
Return the word with the greater length.
patternExplanation: Similar to finding a max number, reduce can be used to select a single 'best' string based on criteria like length.
